chain.thought: The question is asking to identify which event category (conference) has both the highest and lowest number of bids. Since each row represents a conference and the "# of bids" column indicates the number of bids for each conference, we need to group by the "# of bids" column to find the conferences with the maximum and minimum number of bids. We can use the f_group_column(# of bids) operation to group the conferences based on their number of bids to analyze this situation.

The question is asking to identify which event category has both the most and the fewest number of bids. To find this out, we need to analyze the "# of bids" column. Since we are looking for both the highest and lowest number of bids, we should sort the data using the f_sort_column(# of bids) operation. We'll need to sort it twice – once in "large to small" order to find the most bids, and once in "small to large" order to find the fewest bids. This will allow us to see the range of bids across all event categories.
The category with the most number of bids is "southeastern" with 6 bids, and the categories with the fewest number of bids (1 bid) are "colonial" and "western athletic".
